customer_anxiety_profile: Low anxiety, cooperative tone. Wilbert is not hostile to Ion but is passive — he agreed to the visit without strong conviction. The competitor decision was driven by his son, not by Wilbert himself, which means Wilbert's personal buy-in to the existing choice is unclear.
unresolved_concerns: Son is the decision-maker and drove the competitor selection — he will not be present at the visit and his availability for any follow-up is unknown.
Son holds the utility account; bill collection and utility verification are not possible with Wilbert alone.
No qualification data was collected: no bill amount, no utility company, no roof age, no credit qualifier, no prior design details from the competitor.
sensitive_topics_raised: Family already signed with a competitor approximately one week ago — Wilbert framed this as the son's decision.
Wilbert expects to take over the utility account after solar is installed — current account is in the son's name.
exact_promises_rep_made: A specialist will stop by Saturday the 22nd at 12PM noon.
The visit will be a design comparison — Ion's design shown against the competitor's.
If Wilbert likes the design, Ion will find a way to get the information to his son as well.
closer_should_know: Son is the utilities account holder and drove the competitor decision — he was not included in this appointment and Wilbert stated he would not be present 'whatever day you come.' Closer should assess son's availability and involvement on arrival: whether the son can be reached by phone during the visit, whether there is any path to a joint review before a decision is made, and whether Wilbert has any authority to act independently. No utility data or bill was collected — closer will need to obtain this on site or via the son.
